topic: A person discovers their mentor has retired without saying goodbye
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with the dull thud of an unwelcome stone. It was brief, sterile, and utterly devoid of warmth: "Please accept this as notification that I have officially retired, effective immediately." I stared at the screen, my jaw tightening. Ten years of late nights, of whispered advice in the quiet hours, all culminating in this abrupt, impersonal severance. I felt a strange, hollow vibration in my chest, like a tuning fork struck once and then silenced. I didn't bother replying. Why would I waste breath on a courtesy when the gesture felt so utterly meaningless? I closed the laptop with a sharp snap, the sound echoing loudly in the otherwise silent office. The air in the room suddenly felt too thin, too sharp.

[story 2]

I watched him from across the crowded reception area, the way he nodded politely to each visitor, a practiced, smooth motion that didn't quite reach his eyes. When the official announcement finally trickled out, it was delivered with the same flat monotone he used for quarterly reports. A muscle in my neck twitched involuntarily. I took a slow sip of my lukewarm coffee, letting the bitterness coat my tongue. It tasted like ash. I didn't look up when he finally turned toward me, his expression neutral, professional. I simply shifted my weight, crossing my arms tightly over my chest, a deliberate barrier erected between us. My gaze swept over the room, cataloging every detail, making sure he understood the precise measure of my present indifference.

[story 3]

The news hit me during a late-afternoon walk through the familiar park where we used to dissect strategy. I stopped dead, the sound of the distant traffic suddenly grating on my nerves. He was sitting on a bench, reading a paperback, looking entirely too relaxed for a man who had just declared himself done. A tight knot formed low in my stomach, a cold, unpleasant sensation that refused to dissipate. I walked past, my footsteps deliberately measured and heavy, making sure he registered my passage. I didn't slow down, didn't offer a greeting, didn't even spare a glance. The silence between us stretched, thick and heavy, a tangible weight pressing down on the space where our shared history used to reside.
